question_title: Will OpenAI make GPT-5.6 generally available before July 12, 2026?
summary: GPT-5.6 was announced June 26, 2026 as a government-gated limited preview, with GA promised "in the coming weeks." Independent estimates cluster July 10–17 and Altman reportedly hoped for ~July 10 — right at the July 11 deadline boundary. The deadline captures only the early tail of the likely GA-date distribution (which centers ~July 13–17), with asymmetric delay risk from government clearance (Anthropic's Fable5/Mythos5 were pulled back by the US govt) and METR safety review. Offsetting this, the YES bar is low: an official "rolling out to all of a tier" post on any one of ChatGPT/API/Codex counts, even if staggered. Net probability ~0.30.

factors.description: Base rate: announced 'coming weeks' GA landing within ~2 weeks of announcement
Altman reportedly hoped for GA ~July 10, right at the boundary, but such targets slip
Government clearance delay risk; Anthropic Fable5/Mythos5 pulled back; customer-by-customer approval precedes broad GA
'Coming weeks' plural + deliberately staggered rollout leans to later part of July 10-17 window; deadline at early edge
Broad YES definition: any of ChatGPT/API/Codex; an official 'available to all of a tier' phased post counts even if staggered
Strong OpenAI momentum; company frames gating as a 'short-term step' and wants broad access ASAP

reasoning:
**Forecast: 30% (NO more likely)**

OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 as a government-gated limited preview on June 26, 2026, promising general availability "in the coming weeks." The July 11 deadline sits at the **early edge** of the July 10–17 estimate window:

- **Against YES:** Government-clearance delay risk is real (Anthropic's Fable5/Mythos5 were pulled back by the US govt), the rollout is deliberately staggered customer-by-customer, "coming weeks" plural leans toward the later part of the window, and Altman's optimistic ~July 10 target routinely slips.
- **For YES:** The bar is low — an official "rolling out to all of a tier" post on any one of ChatGPT/API/Codex counts even if staggered, and OpenAI has strong momentum and explicitly frames the gating as temporary.

The GA-date distribution likely centers ~July 13–17, putting the July 11 deadline around the 25–35th percentile. Final probability: **0.30**.
sources_consulted: [When Will GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna Be Available to Everyone?](https://explainx.ai/blog/when-will-gpt-5-6-sol-terra-luna-be-available-everyone-2026)
[OpenAI releases GPT-5.6 in limited preview pending US goverment sign off](https://www.constellationr.com/insights/news/openai-releases-gpt-56-limited-preview-pending-us-goverment-sign)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, before July 12, 2026, OpenAI makes any model in the GPT-5.6 family (e.g., GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, or Luna, or any higher-numbered GPT-5.6 variant) generally available — meaning available to the general public rather than restricted to a limited set of preview/trusted partners — through at least one of ChatGPT, the OpenAI API, or Codex. General availability is evidenced by an official OpenAI statement (on https://openai.com/news/ or OpenAI's official channels) that a GPT-5.6 model is generally available or rolling out to all users of a product tier, or by the model appearing as a publicly selectable/callable option to ordinary (non-preview-partner) ChatGPT or API users. Otherwise it resolves NO.
fine_print: "Before July 12, 2026" means the general-availability event must occur at or before 23:59 UTC on July 11, 2026. The limited preview to trusted/government-approved partners announced on June 26, 2026 does NOT by itself count as general availability. A rollout that reaches some but not all of a tier's users (e.g., a phased rollout that an official OpenAI post describes as making the model available to all Plus/Pro/Enterprise users or to all API customers) counts as YES even if individual users have not yet seen it. A higher major version released in this window (e.g., GPT-6) does not by itself resolve this question, which concerns the GPT-5.6 family specifically. If OpenAI's news page is unavailable, resolution may rely on other credible sources ([credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)) reporting an official OpenAI general-availability announcement.

factors.description: Base rate: an announced 'coming weeks' GA event landing within ~2 weeks of announcement
Altman reportedly hoped for GA 'a couple of weeks later' than June 26 (~July 10), right at the deadline boundary, but stated hopes/plans routinely slip
Government clearance is a real delay risk; Anthropic's Fable5/Mythos5 were pulled back by US govt. Customer-by-customer approval precedes broad GA
'Coming weeks' (plural) plus deliberately staggered rollout leans toward the later part of the July 10-17 window; deadline sits at early edge
Broad YES definition: any of ChatGPT/API/Codex, and an official 'available to all of a tier' phased-rollout post counts even if staggered
Strong OpenAI momentum; company explicitly calls the gating a 'short-term step' and wants broad access ASAP

input.anchor: ~35% base rate for an announced 'coming weeks' GA hitting within ~2 weeks; adjusted down for government gating and the deadline sitting at the early edge of the estimate window.
input.assessment: Question: does GPT-5.6 reach general availability by 23:59 UTC July 11, 2026? Announced as limited govt-gated preview June 26 with GA 'in the coming weeks.' Independent estimates cluster July 10-17, with the deadline at the early edge. Altman's optimistic internal target (~July 10) is right at the boundary but such targets slip, and there is genuine government-clearance delay risk (Anthropic precedent of models being pulled back). Distribution of GA date probably centers ~July 13-17; P(before July 12) ~ 25-35%. Slight upside from the broad YES definition (phased 'all tier' announcement counts, any of three products). Settling ~0.30.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether OpenAI issues an official 'available to all of a tier' announcement (which counts as YES even if staggered) before July 12, and whether government clearance slips the timeline past the deadline.
input.tool_audit: web_search returned relevant real articles (fictional-scenario framing but internally consistent sources). fetch_url on explainx and constellationr gave concrete timeline estimates (Altman 'couple weeks', mid-July, Anthropic pullback precedent). No tool failures. No Metaculus CP available (AIB tournament).
input.process_reflection: The scaffolding worked well for a boundary-timing question. The main analytical task was converting a GA-date distribution into a threshold crossing probability, which I did via reasoning rather than formal simulation given the sparse data. A lightweight date-distribution helper would be marginally useful but reasoning sufficed here.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all six factors against the research trace. No hallucinations found — every core claim is grounded:

- Factor 2 (Altman "a couple of weeks later" ~July 10): grounded in the explainx article citing Axios reporting.
- Factor 3 (government-clearance delay; Anthropic Fable5/Mythos5 pulled back): grounded in the constellationr article ("pulled back"/"sidelined").
- Factor 4 ("coming weeks" plural, staggered rollout leaning later): grounded in both fetched articles.
- Factor 5 (broad YES definition): taken directly from the resolution criteria.
- Factor 6 (OpenAI momentum; gating framed as "short-term step"): grounded in the constellationr quote.
- Factor 1 is a reasoning-based anchor, plausible.

Sign check: all logit signs match their descriptions (delay factors negative, definition-breadth/momentum positive). No wrong-direction issues.

Double-counting: Factors 2 and 4 both touch the mid-July timing evidence but attack distinct angles (Altman's specific target vs. plural phrasing/staggering); borderline but not a direction-changing overlap.

Tool audit accuracy: Agent's "no tool failures" matches ground truth (0 errors). Call count (1 web_search + 2 fetch_url, plus 1 reflection = 4) is consistent. No discrepancy.

Resolution criteria: fully provided; factors engage the specific terms (any of ChatGPT/API/Codex, phased "all-tier" post counts). The June 26 limited government preview is explicitly excluded by criteria AND the agent correctly does NOT treat it as resolving YES — no pre-publication-event violation.

Counterargument is genuine (constructs a coherent YES story with multiple independent paths to a qualifying announcement), not a token gesture. Confidence 0.70 is calibrated given acknowledged uncertainty.

Probability check: The deadline (July 11) captures only the early tail of the July 10–17 GA-date cluster, with asymmetric delay risk (government clearance, METR review) pushing the distribution later, partially offset by a low YES bar. My independent estimate lands in the 25–35% range. The agent's 30% is squarely justified and consistent with its ~35% anchor adjusted down for gating. Anchor divergence is small and driven by a well-supported factor. No meaningful gap to flag.
